schema_version: v0 — component-charter shape; migrates into the intention envelope when MAP.md Q6 lands
what: Cairn's launch/rescue scripts — canonical here, symlinked onto PATH. Currently one: superclaude, the Claude Code launcher.
why: A single, self-describing entry point to start Claude Code with Cairn's standing session contract, so a launch is reproducible and its settings live in code, not in someone's shell history. The rescue ethos (carried from the quarry, re-derived not grafted): from parse to exec, nothing may abort — always reach Claude Code so it can help rebuild a broken box.
state: v0 — first cast 2026-07-15. Lean by design: none of the quarry launcher's rack-server / tmux / wizard / credential machinery — added only if a measured need appears.

context_switch.shape: a two-state switch (1M / 200K) where the COMMAND LINE ALWAYS WINS.
context_switch.why: Big-design stretches want 1M; heavy-buildout stretches default to 200K — the real why beyond convenience: Claude cannot self-trigger /compact, so at 1M context balloons past Akien's notification threshold before he is alerted; defaulting to 200K makes stewardship a system property (physics, Law 4), not a thing to remember (discipline). Flipping the built-in default is a one-line edit (FILE_DEFAULT_200K); flags always override it.
context_switch.current_default: 200K (FILE_DEFAULT_200K=1) — set 2026-07-15 for the buildout stretch (ticket default-superclaude-to-200k); --1m opts up.
context_switch.flags: --1m unsets the constraint (opts UP, wins even over a 200K environment); --200k imposes it. unset — NOT =0: the disable var only ever turns 1M OFF, so =0 is a no-op that looks like it worked.
context_switch.precedence: command line > environment > file default > unconstrained (1M)
context_switch.measured_caveat: docs say unset makes 1M *available* while the [1m] model variant *selects* it; empirically unset yields 1M here (the writing session was 1M). If a --1m launch ever comes up 200K, set SUPERCLAUDE_1M_MODEL / ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_OPUS_MODEL to a [1m] variant — not before (measure, don't pre-engineer).

superclaude.passthrough: all other args forwarded to claude unchanged
superclaude.prove: --dry-run prints the resolved context state + command without launching
superclaude.env_var_provenance: CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_1M_CONTEXT — from the quarry's own superclaude (UnseenUniversity/bin/superclaude), run for months; corroborated against `claude --help` + Claude Code docs (the 1M control is an env var, not a flag).
superclaude.meta_lesson: The quarry launcher carried this exact two-state logic but NOT its intent/why — so it was misread on first contact (the toggle was reverse-engineered backwards). This file now writes the why IN, a live case of the 2026-07-15 naming stone: the intent+why belong at the address, and their absence cost real work.

path_wiring: ~/.local/bin/superclaude -> this file (that dir holds the claude-family PATH links); repo-root cairn/superclaude -> launchers/superclaude (a finder pointer, matching past practice).
traces_to: Law 5 (this component carries its own why)
the rescue ethos — always reach the tool that can help rebuild
owner: Akien owns the launch contract (what's always-on is his call); CC drafts.
proof: hand-run: `superclaude --dry-run` resolves the expected command; a real launch lands in a 1M/200K session per the flag, named + remote + dangerous as specified. Recorded in proofs/.
provenance: born 2026-07-15 by Akien's spec; reimplemented fresh (not grafted) from the quarry launcher's proven interface.
